steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize the order in which the two lighthouse keepers began their careers — specifically when each one started, not when they finished.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Before automation, lighthouse keepers like Nora Whitfield and Clara J. Morgan were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.: Does not specify when either keeper began, so it cannot establish the order of career starts. ✗
  • Choice B — Clara J. Morgan began her career as a lighthouse keeper years after Nora Whitfield did.: Directly compares when each career started, showing that Nora began first and the other keeper began years later. ✓
  • Choice C — Nora Whitfield's career as a lighthouse keeper ended in 1857, whereas Clara J. Morgan's ended in 1917.: Shifts focus to when careers ended rather than when they began. ✗
  • Choice D — From 1834 to 1857, the nighttime waters of Maryland were more navigable thanks to lighthouse keepers Clara J. Morgan and Nora Whitfield.: Contains details that contradict the passage, making it factually wrong. ✗
B is correct.
